taxation_system.social_security: Depends on whether income is treated as employment, self-employment, local business income or foreign-source remote work. Digital nomad permits often do not equal local social-insurance coverage.
taxation_system.notes: Tax system summary is for screening. Actual liability depends on residence, source of income, employment/self-employment structure, entity setup, social security and treaty position.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Screening summary for a foreign-contract remote worker/digital nomad. It is not tax advice. Verify official tax, social-security and immigration fee pages before relying on any amount.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Naturalisation usually requires long-term residence permit/settled residence, 8 years residence including 5 years permanent/long-term residence, Estonian language B1, constitution/citizenship exam, legal income, loyalty oath.

permanent_residence_years.notes: Approximate PR/permanent-residence stage before citizenship; verify route-specific counting.

key_conditions.value: Naturalisation usually requires long-term residence permit/settled residence, 8 years residence including 5 years permanent/long-term residence, Estonian language B1, constitution/citizenship exam, legal income, loyalty oath.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Restricted for naturalised citizens; Estonia generally does not allow voluntary dual citizenship for adults.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The stay route may be called residence, but current evidence is weak that it reliably leads to PR/citizenship. Treat as live/work option first, citizenship option only after legal confirmation.

data.notes: Official digital-nomad and tax points verified; citizenship summarized from secondary source.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: No separate fast marriage citizenship track confirmed, or marriage mainly helps residence.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to an Estonian citizen can support family residence, but it does not create automatic citizenship or a materially short naturalisation track. Same-sex marriage is legal since 2024.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

benefit_to_migrant_father.value: A child born in Estonia is not automatically Estonian unless citizenship-by-descent or stateless-child rules apply; birth mainly helps family-life residence arguments, not father citizenship.
benefit_to_migrant_father.notes: This is about whether a locally born child helps the migrant father; it is not legal advice.

if_both_parents_migrants.value: Child normally does not receive citizenship merely by local birth if both parents are foreign temporary migrants, except statelessness/special rules.

if_second_parent_local_citizen.value: If the other parent is a local citizen, the child usually receives citizenship by descent or birth registration. This can support the migrant father’s family residence, but does not automatically grant father citizenship.

parent_benefit_summary.value: A child born in Estonia is not automatically Estonian unless citizenship-by-descent or stateless-child rules apply; birth mainly helps family-life residence arguments, not father citizenship.

child.notes: A child born in Estonia is not automatically Estonian unless citizenship-by-descent or stateless-child rules apply; birth mainly helps family-life residence arguments, not father citizenship.

passport.strength_notes: Strong EU passport. Citizenship requires Estonian language and constitutional knowledge; e-Residency and digital nomad visa do not reduce the citizenship clock.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Strong EU passport. Citizenship requires Estonian language and constitutional knowledge; e-Residency and digital nomad visa do not reduce the citizenship clock.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Argentina is a special case: federal court citizenship can generally be requested after 2 years living in Argentina. The stronger practical case has legal residence/DNI, lawful income, Spanish documents, clean records and real center of life.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: For the applicant, Argentina is one of the best citizenship-oriented options if he actually relocates: 2 years residence before federal court, Spanish-language paperwork, proof of honest livelihood, Argentine criminal record and foreign criminal record. Digital nomad/transitory status alone is weaker than converting into a regular residence/DNI setup.
key_conditions.notes: Recent political proposals/tightening should be monitored; do not rely on birth-tourism style entry without genuine residence.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.value: Digital Nomad Visa / temporary remote-worker stay
citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Argentina effectively tolerates dual citizenship and does not generally allow renunciation for many purposes.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Commonly 12-36+ months after filing, depending on federal court, documents, prosecutor objections and proof of real residence.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Court practice varies materially.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The selected route is PR/residence-oriented or can plausibly be converted/maintained as qualifying residence for naturalisation if the applicant actually relocates and satisfies residence, language, tax and continuity rules.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Marriage is not a separate automatic statutory passport route; it helps prove arraigo/family ties and may make residence/court case easier.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to an Argentine citizen helps mainly as evidence of arraigo and may support residence; it does not automatically bypass the need to live in Argentina and file naturalisation before a federal judge. Fake marriage or paper-only residence can backfire because courts increasingly look for real center of life.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: Existing marriage to a non-Argentine spouse mainly matters for dependent residence. Marriage to an Argentine citizen is helpful only if genuine and accompanied by real residence in Argentina.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

birthright_citizenship.notes: Any child born in Argentina is Argentine at birth except children of foreign diplomats/foreign government service.

benefit_to_migrant_father.value: A child born in Argentina is one of the strongest family anchors in the dataset: it can support residence and strengthen the father’s 2-year citizenship court case, but the father still must show real residence, livelihood and documents.

if_both_parents_migrants.value: If both parents are foreign migrants, the child born in Argentina is still Argentine by birth, except narrow diplomatic exceptions.

if_second_parent_local_citizen.value: If the other parent is Argentine, the child is Argentine by birth/descent. For the migrant father this is strong family evidence but still not automatic citizenship.

parent_benefit_summary.value: A child born in Argentina is Argentine by jus soli; for a migrant father this is one of the strongest family anchors and may help residence/citizenship strategy.

child.notes: A child born in Argentina is Argentine by jus soli; for a migrant father this is one of the strongest family anchors and may help residence/citizenship strategy.

passport.strength_notes: Strong South American passport; Argentina is one of the fastest citizenship jurisdictions on paper, but court practice and documentation matter.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to an Armenian citizen can reduce/alter requirements after a qualifying marriage/residence period; genuineness and documentation matter. No automatic citizenship by marriage.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

if_both_parents_migrants.value: Child normally does not receive citizenship merely by local birth if both parents are foreign temporary migrants, except statelessness/special rules.

if_second_parent_local_citizen.value: If the other parent is a local citizen, the child usually receives citizenship by descent or birth registration. This can support the migrant father’s family residence, but does not automatically grant father citizenship.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage/de facto partnership with an Australian can create partner visa pathways, but citizenship still requires PR, residence and character/test rules. No automatic citizenship by marriage.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

benefit_to_migrant_father.value: A child born in Australia is citizen only if at least one parent is citizen/PR, or after ordinarily residing in Australia until age 10; birth alone usually does not help a temporary migrant father immediately.
benefit_to_migrant_father.notes: This is about whether a locally born child helps the migrant father; it is not legal advice.

if_both_parents_migrants.value: Child may receive citizenship only if parent residence/domicile/status conditions are met; two temporary migrants should verify before relying on birth.

if_second_parent_local_citizen.value: If the other parent is a local citizen, the child usually receives citizenship by descent or birth registration. This can support the migrant father’s family residence, but does not automatically grant father citizenship.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage/stable union with a Brazilian can reduce naturalisation residence to about 1 year once residence is held; authorities check genuineness and documentation.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

benefit_to_migrant_father.value: A child born in Brazil is Brazilian by jus soli and can strongly support family residence and shortened naturalisation for the father.
benefit_to_migrant_father.notes: This is about whether a locally born child helps the migrant father; it is not legal advice.

if_both_parents_migrants.value: Child normally receives citizenship by birth in the country, except narrow diplomatic/other exceptions. This can materially improve family residence strategy for parents.

if_second_parent_local_citizen.value: If the other parent is a local citizen, the child usually receives citizenship by descent or birth registration. This can support the migrant father’s family residence, but does not automatically grant father citizenship.

parent_benefit_summary.value: A child born in Brazil is Brazilian by jus soli and can strongly support family residence and shortened naturalisation for the father.

child.notes: A child born in Brazil is Brazilian by jus soli and can strongly support family residence and shortened naturalisation for the father.

passport.strength_notes: Strong regional/global passport. Brazil is family-friendly for citizenship because Brazilian spouse/child can reduce the naturalisation period.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Strong regional/global passport. Brazil is family-friendly for citizenship because Brazilian spouse/child can reduce the naturalisation period.

practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Typical path requires years of lawful residence, permanent/long-term residence, Bulgarian language, income, clean record and release/dual-citizenship analysis. Digital nomad permit does not automatically shorten this.

permanent_residence_years.notes: Approximate PR/permanent-residence stage before citizenship; verify route-specific counting.

key_conditions.value: Typical path requires years of lawful residence, permanent/long-term residence, Bulgarian language, income, clean record and release/dual-citizenship analysis. Digital nomad permit does not automatically shorten this.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship rules are restrictive for some naturalising adults, with exceptions; verify by nationality.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The country has a useful independent route, but citizenship usually requires converting from nomad/freelance status into ordinary residence/permanent residence or proving the status counts as qualifying residence.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Bulgarian citizen can reduce naturalisation conditions after a qualifying marriage period, but residence, language and character requirements still matter.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

benefit_to_migrant_father.value: Birth in Bulgaria generally does not give citizenship unless a parent is Bulgarian or statelessness rules apply; a child born there rarely helps a foreign father directly.
benefit_to_migrant_father.notes: This is about whether a locally born child helps the migrant father; it is not legal advice.

if_both_parents_migrants.value: Child normally does not receive citizenship merely by local birth if both parents are foreign temporary migrants, except statelessness/special rules.

if_second_parent_local_citizen.value: If the other parent is a local citizen, the child usually receives citizenship by descent or birth registration. This can support the migrant father’s family residence, but does not automatically grant father citizenship.

parent_benefit_summary.value: Birth in Bulgaria generally does not give citizenship unless a parent is Bulgarian or statelessness rules apply; a child born there rarely helps a foreign father directly.

child.notes: Birth in Bulgaria generally does not give citizenship unless a parent is Bulgarian or statelessness rules apply; a child born there rarely helps a foreign father directly.

data.selection_summary: Chile does have temporary residence subcategories that looked relevant on re-check, but none is a clean fit for ordinary foreign-contract remote work. The own-account/remunerated-activities route requires a Chilean or Chile permanent-resident counterparty contract for professional/specialized services over 90 days; rentista requires passive income from real estate or financial assets; investor requires a qualifying Chile investment/business basis. No dedicated digital-nomad/foreign-client remote-work route was confirmed.

rejected_routes.route_name: Temporary residence for lawful remunerated activities - own-account/services

minimum_monthly_income_usd.notes: No generic digital-nomad income threshold. Route depends on Chile-linked contract and counterparty capacity/solvency.

minimum_income_local_currency.value: No fixed amount captured; must support contractual obligations / counterparty solvency.

income_requirement_display.value: Not a foreign-client nomad route: requires service/professional contract with Chilean or Chile permanent-resident counterparty for >90 days.
income_requirement_display.local_or_formula_value: Chile-linked service contract; no generic nomad threshold.

income_proof_months.display_value: Not specified as months; documentation is contract/counterparty solvency.

key_requirements.requirement: For own-account services, before entering Chile the foreigner must conclude a civil/commercial contract with a Chilean or foreigner holding definitive residence.
The services must be immaterial or specialized/professional and exceed 90 consecutive days.

initial_validity.value: Up to 2 years under general temporary residence rules
initial_validity.notes: General temporary residence rule; category-specific grant may vary.

extension_rules.value: Can generally be extended successively for two years, except listed exceptions.

rejected_routes.rejection_reason: Requires Chile-linked client/counterparty contract, not ordinary foreign-contract remote work.

key_requirements.requirement: Rentistas must have constant income from real estate or financial assets.
Income must be regular and sufficient to satisfy at least basic needs during the stay.

initial_validity.value: Up to 2 years under general temporary residence rules

extension_rules.value: Renewal possible if rentista condition and sufficient resources are maintained.

data.selection_summary: Colombia has a recognized digital-nomad/remote-worker visitor visa route. It is one of the stronger fits in this batch: foreign employment or independent digital services, health insurance, and income around three Colombian minimum monthly wages are commonly cited; the stay can be up to about two years, but it is not itself a direct PR/citizenship route.

best_routes.route_name: Visa V - Digital Nomad / remote worker

minimum_monthly_income_usd.notes: USD estimate depends on current Colombian minimum wage and exchange rate; keep local formula authoritative.

minimum_income_local_currency.value: 3 Colombian minimum monthly legal wages
minimum_income_local_currency.notes: USD estimate depends on current Colombian minimum wage and exchange rate; keep local formula authoritative.

income_proof_months.notes: Secondary visa summaries commonly cite bank statements for recent months; official cleanup needed.

key_requirements.requirement: Proof of remote work or digital services for foreign employers/clients or business outside Colombia.
Health insurance covering the stay in Colombia.
Income at or above the cited minimum-wage multiple.

initial_validity.value: Up to 2 years commonly cited for the visitor digital nomad category

extension_rules.notes: Use official visa portal for renewal/extension details.

path_to_temporary_residence.value: Visitor visa permits lawful temporary stay for the granted period.

path_to_permanent_residence.notes: Visitor visa category generally does not directly create PR eligibility; separate migrant/resident categories may be needed. Settlement-track review: The country has a useful independent route, but citizenship usually requires converting from nomad/freelance status into ordinary residence/permanent residence or proving the status counts as qualifying residence.

path_to_citizenship.value: Uncertain; likely requires conversion or legal confirmation that this status counts.
path_to_citizenship.notes: The country has a useful independent route, but citizenship usually requires converting from nomad/freelance status into ordinary residence/permanent residence or proving the status counts as qualifying residence.

best_routes.notes: Strong near-term remote-work option; weaker for long-term settlement unless combined with another Colombian category later.

income_requirement_display.value: USD 1,100 / month
income_requirement_display.local_or_formula_value: 3 Colombian minimum monthly legal wages
income_requirement_display.notes: USD estimate depends on current Colombian minimum wage and exchange rate; keep local formula authoritative.

income_tax_rate_percent.notes: Top marginal PIT rate for fiscal residents. Resident general/pension/dividend basket rates are progressive by UVT: 0%, 19%, 28%, 33%, 35%, 37%, 39%. Use this as top marginal rate for dashboard filtering, not expected effective rate.

tax_residency_rule.value: 183 days in Colombia, continuously or not, within any 365-day period; if the period ends in the second year, residence applies in that second year.
tax_residency_rule.notes: Colombian nationals also have additional residence tests based on spouse/dependants, Colombian-source income, Colombian-managed assets, Colombian-located assets, proof of foreign residence, or tax-haven residence.

foreign_income_taxation.value: Fiscal residents are taxed on worldwide income and should report assets/equity in Colombia and abroad. Non-residents are taxed only on Colombian-source income and report Colombian equity/assets only.
foreign_income_taxation.notes: Important for digital nomads: staying under 183 days may avoid Colombian fiscal residence, but Colombian-source income and local activity still need review.

social_security.value: Colombian employment contracts trigger mandatory social security. PwC cites total employee/employer contributions around 28.5% of monthly salary, with employee portions generally 4% health and 4% pension, plus solidarity contributions above thresholds. Foreign remote-worker/contractor treatment requires local advice.
social_security.notes: For a foreign employer/client arrangement, do not assume local payroll social security applies automatically; structure matters.

special_regimes.value: No confirmed digital-nomad tax holiday or foreign-income exemption found. Treat Colombia as residence-based taxation once fiscal residence is triggered.
special_regimes.notes: Visa eligibility and tax residence are separate.

income_tax_rate_display.value: Progressive 0-39% for fiscal residents; non-residents taxed only on Colombian-source income.
income_tax_rate_display.notes: A foreign remote worker on a digital nomad visa can become tax resident after the day-count test; no blanket digital-nomad income-tax exemption was confirmed.

other_relevant_taxes.value: General VAT 19%/5%; financial transactions tax 0.4%; capital gains generally 15% except lottery/gaming 20%; equity tax can apply above high UVT net-worth thresholds.
other_relevant_taxes.notes: Equity tax threshold/rates and 2026 changes require confirmation before high-net-worth planning.

taxes.review_summary: Updated Colombia tax fields from PwC 2026 individual tax summaries. Key change: dashboard numeric tax rate set to top marginal 39%, with notes explaining progressive 0-39% system and 183-day fiscal residence rule.

taxation_system.value: 39% top/screening PIT rate. Progressive 0-39% for fiscal residents; non-residents taxed only on Colombian-source income.. Residence: 183 days in Colombia, continuously or not, within any 365-day period; if the period ends in the second year, residence applies in that second year.. Income scope: Fiscal residents are taxed on worldwide income and should report assets/equity in Colombia and abroad. Non-residents are taxed only on Colombian-source income and report Colombian equity/assets only.. Special regimes: No confirmed digital-nomad tax holiday or foreign-income exemption found. Treat Colombia as residence-based taxation once fiscal residence is triggered.. Social/payroll: Colombian employment contracts trigger mandatory social security. PwC cites total employee/employer contributions around 28.5% of monthly salary, with employee portions generally 4% health and 4% pension, plus solidarity contributions above thresholds. Foreign remote-worker/contractor treatment requires local advice.
taxation_system.tax_residency_rule: 183 days in Colombia, continuously or not, within any 365-day period; if the period ends in the second year, residence applies in that second year.
taxation_system.income_scope: Fiscal residents are taxed on worldwide income and should report assets/equity in Colombia and abroad. Non-residents are taxed only on Colombian-source income and report Colombian equity/assets only.
taxation_system.special_regimes: No confirmed digital-nomad tax holiday or foreign-income exemption found. Treat Colombia as residence-based taxation once fiscal residence is triggered.
taxation_system.social_security: Colombian employment contracts trigger mandatory social security. PwC cites total employee/employer contributions around 28.5% of monthly salary, with employee portions generally 4% health and 4% pension, plus solidarity contributions above thresholds. Foreign remote-worker/contractor treatment requires local advice.
taxation_system.notes: Tax system summary is for screening. Actual liability depends on residence, source of income, employment/self-employment structure, entity setup, social security and treaty position.

digital_nomad_taxation.value: Colombia: digital_nomad_or_remote_worker_visa. 39% top/screening PIT rate. Progressive 0-39% for fiscal residents; non-residents taxed only on Colombian-source income. Tax residence: 183 days in Colombia, continuously or not, within any 365-day period; if the period ends in the second year, residence applies in that second year.. Foreign income: Fiscal residents are taxed on worldwide income and should report assets/equity in Colombia and abroad. Non-residents are taxed only on Colombian-source income and report Colombian equity/assets only.. Digital-nomad/special regimes: No confirmed digital-nomad tax holiday or foreign-income exemption found. Treat Colombia as residence-based taxation once fiscal residence is triggered.. Fixed payments: possible_or_route_dependent_fixed_payments; Fixed payments may exist, but treatment depends on residence, self-employment registration, local payroll, health insurance and whether income is local-source or foreign-source.

digital_nomad_taxation.special_digital_nomad_or_inbound_regime: No confirmed digital-nomad tax holiday or foreign-income exemption found. Treat Colombia as residence-based taxation once fiscal residence is triggered.
digital_nomad_taxation.social_security_and_payroll: Colombian employment contracts trigger mandatory social security. PwC cites total employee/employer contributions around 28.5% of monthly salary, with employee portions generally 4% health and 4% pension, plus solidarity contributions above thresholds. Foreign remote-worker/contractor treatment requires local advice.

fixed_payments.social_security_or_payroll: Colombian employment contracts trigger mandatory social security. PwC cites total employee/employer contributions around 28.5% of monthly salary, with employee portions generally 4% health and 4% pension, plus solidarity contributions above thresholds. Foreign remote-worker/contractor treatment requires local advice.
fixed_payments.visa_or_state_fees: Not captured in tax fields. Visa/application/card fees may still apply and should be checked on the official route page.
fixed_payments.other_fixed_or_transaction_taxes: General VAT 19%/5%; financial transactions tax 0.4%; capital gains generally 15% except lottery/gaming 20%; equity tax can apply above high UVT net-worth thresholds.
fixed_payments.notes: Fixed payments may exist, but treatment depends on residence, self-employment registration, local payroll, health insurance and whether income is local-source or foreign-source.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Screening summary for a foreign-contract remote worker/digital nomad. It is not tax advice. Verify official tax, social-security and immigration fee pages before relying on any amount.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Naturalisation timing depends on nationality and residence category: often 5 years for foreigners, 2 years for Latin American/Caribbean/Spanish nationals or spouses/parents of Colombians, 1 year for some Latin American nationals. Confirm whether V nomad time counts.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Naturalisation timing depends on nationality and residence category: often 5 years for foreigners, 2 years for Latin American/Caribbean/Spanish nationals or spouses/parents of Colombians, 1 year for some Latin American nationals. Confirm whether V nomad time counts.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.value: Visa V - Digital Nomad / remote worker
citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The country has a useful independent route, but citizenship usually requires converting from nomad/freelance status into ordinary residence/permanent residence or proving the status counts as qualifying residence.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage or permanent partnership with a Colombian can reduce residence requirement, but a qualifying resident visa/status and genuine relationship are essential.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Screening summary for a foreign-contract remote worker/digital nomad. It is not tax advice. Verify official tax, social-security and immigration fee pages before relying on any amount.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Naturalisation usually requires 7 years residence, reduced to 5 for Central Americans, Ibero-Americans and Spaniards, plus Spanish/civics and good conduct. Digital nomad stay may not equal ordinary residence.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Naturalisation usually requires 7 years residence, reduced to 5 for Central Americans, Ibero-Americans and Spaniards, plus Spanish/civics and good conduct. Digital nomad stay may not equal ordinary residence.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is generally tolerated.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The route is mainly a temporary digital-nomad/workcation/visitor stay. It should not be ranked as a citizenship path unless the applicant later switches to a separate residence/PR route.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Costa Rican can allow naturalisation after 2 years of marriage and 2 years residence, but relationship and residence must be real.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Costa Rica has broad birthright/registration rules; a Costa Rican child can help family residence and may shorten family-based strategy, but does not automatically naturalise the father.

passport.strength_notes: Strong Central American passport. Digital nomad stay is attractive, but citizenship requires real residence and Spanish/civics integration.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The country has a useful independent route, but citizenship usually requires converting from nomad/freelance status into ordinary residence/permanent residence or proving the status counts as qualifying residence.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: No separate fast marriage citizenship track confirmed, or marriage mainly helps residence.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Czech citizen helps residence strategy but does not by itself create a short automatic citizenship route; residence and integration remain central.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Czechia does not automatically give citizenship unless a parent is Czech or statelessness rules apply.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The selected route is PR/residence-oriented or can plausibly be converted/maintained as qualifying residence for naturalisation if the applicant actually relocates and satisfies residence, language, tax and continuity rules.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage or stable union with an Ecuadorian can shorten naturalisation/residence strategy, but relationship and legal residence must be documented.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: A child born in Ecuador is Ecuadorian by jus soli and can be a strong family anchor for residence and possibly reduced naturalisation timing for the father.

passport.strength_notes: Moderate passport with regional mobility. Ecuador has a relatively short naturalisation period if temporary residence converts into qualifying residence.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Moderate passport with regional mobility. Ecuador has a relatively short naturalisation period if temporary residence converts into qualifying residence.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: Spanish is the practical daily/legal language; English can work in tech/expat circles, but immigration, courts and tax filings are mostly Spanish.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: No general official support assumed; use only expat/community resources unless separately verified.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Ordinary naturalisation usually requires 7 years legal residence, permanent/long-term status where applicable, Greek language/culture/economic-social integration and clean record.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Ordinary naturalisation usually requires 7 years legal residence, permanent/long-term status where applicable, Greek language/culture/economic-social integration and clean record.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The country has a useful independent route, but citizenship usually requires converting from nomad/freelance status into ordinary residence/permanent residence or proving the status counts as qualifying residence.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Greek citizen can help residence and may reduce timing when there is a Greek child, but no automatic citizenship by marriage alone.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Greece gives citizenship only under conditions such as parents long-term lawful residence/schooling; a Greek child can materially help family residence and possibly timing.

passport.strength_notes: EU passport. Digital nomad residence may be useful, but citizenship requires Greek integration and exams/interview.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: EU passport. Digital nomad residence may be useful, but citizenship requires Greek integration and exams/interview.

visa_free_destinations.notes: Henley Passport Index 2026 visa-free / visa-on-arrival destination count.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: Local official language is important for immigration/citizenship. English may be usable in tech/tourism/expat contexts, but long-term integration normally requires the local language.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: No general official support assumed; use only expat/community resources unless separately verified.

path_to_citizenship.value: Uncertain; likely requires conversion or legal confirmation that this status counts.

tax_residency_rule.value: Tax residence usually depends on day count, habitual home/center of interests, residence permit and treaty facts. For remote contractors, confirm before crossing 183 days or registering locally.
tax_residency_rule.notes: Prompt-fill screening note; country-specific tax advice needed.

foreign_income_taxation.notes: Not deeply researched in this batch; verify residency/treaty effects before planning.

special_regimes.value: Check whether digital nomad exemption, territorial taxation, non-dom/inbound worker regime or freelancer small-business regime applies. Do not assume foreign income is tax-free.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Screening summary for a foreign-contract remote worker/digital nomad. It is not tax advice. Verify official tax, social-security and immigration fee pages before relying on any amount.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Ordinary naturalisation generally requires 8 years residence, clean record, livelihood, housing and Hungarian constitutional/basic knowledge. White Card status may not lead cleanly to permanent residence/citizenship.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Ordinary naturalisation generally requires 8 years residence, clean record, livelihood, housing and Hungarian constitutional/basic knowledge. White Card status may not lead cleanly to permanent residence/citizenship.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The stay route may be called residence, but current evidence is weak that it reliably leads to PR/citizenship. Treat as live/work option first, citizenship option only after legal confirmation.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Hungarian can reduce naturalisation period, especially after 3/5/10 years depending children and duration, but language/constitutional and residence criteria still matter.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Hungary does not usually give citizenship unless a parent is Hungarian or statelessness rules apply; a Hungarian child can reduce marriage-naturalisation timing if one parent is Hungarian.

passport.strength_notes: EU passport. White Card is temporary and explicitly not the same as permanent settlement.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: EU passport. White Card is temporary and explicitly not the same as permanent settlement.

index_notes.value: Passport rank and visa-free count filled from Henley Passport Index 2026 table.

visa_free_destinations.notes: Henley Passport Index 2026 visa-free / visa-on-arrival destination count.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: Local official language is important for immigration/citizenship. English may be usable in tech/tourism/expat contexts, but long-term integration normally requires the local language.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: No general official support assumed; use only expat/community resources unless separately verified.

labor_market.software_engineer_notes: For this selection the applicant works remotely for a foreign client. Local software market is secondary; for long-term settlement, local language, tax registration, and ability to document lawful income matter more than local hiring demand in Hungary.

average_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not filled with a guessed value; prompt asks for reliable source. Needs salary-source cleanup if local employment comparison becomes important.

median_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not filled with a guessed value; reliable median source not captured in this pass.

benefit_to_migrant_father.value: Birth in Hungary does not usually give citizenship unless a parent is Hungarian or statelessness rules apply; a Hungarian child can reduce marriage-naturalisation timing if one parent is Hungarian.
benefit_to_migrant_father.notes: This is about whether a locally born child helps the migrant father; it is not legal advice.

if_both_parents_migrants.value: Child normally does not receive citizenship merely by local birth if both parents are foreign temporary migrants, except statelessness/special rules.

if_second_parent_local_citizen.value: If the other parent is a local citizen, the child usually receives citizenship by descent or birth registration. This can support the migrant father’s family residence, but does not automatically grant father citizenship.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Naturalisation generally requires 5 consecutive or 10 non-consecutive years residence, Indonesian language/Pancasila/constitution, lawful income and renunciation issues. Remote-worker visa must be checked for citizenship-counting.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Naturalisation generally requires 5 consecutive or 10 non-consecutive years residence, Indonesian language/Pancasila/constitution, lawful income and renunciation issues. Remote-worker visa must be checked for citizenship-counting.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.value: Remote Worker Visa / E33G-style limited stay
citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is generally not allowed for adults, with limited child exceptions.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The country has a useful independent route, but citizenship usually requires converting from nomad/freelance status into ordinary residence/permanent residence or proving the status counts as qualifying residence.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to an Indonesian can support stay permits and eventual naturalisation after qualifying residence, but citizenship is not automatic and dual-citizenship restrictions are a major risk.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Indonesia does not normally give citizenship unless descent/statelessness rules apply. Children of mixed marriages have special dual-citizenship rules until adulthood.

passport.strength_notes: Moderate passport. Naturalisation is possible but Indonesia is restrictive on dual citizenship and foreign-worker compliance.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Moderate passport. Naturalisation is possible but Indonesia is restrictive on dual citizenship and foreign-worker compliance.

index_notes.value: Passport rank and visa-free count filled from Henley Passport Index 2026 table.

visa_free_destinations.notes: Henley Passport Index 2026 visa-free / visa-on-arrival destination count.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: Local official language is important for immigration/citizenship. English may be usable in tech/tourism/expat contexts, but long-term integration normally requires the local language.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: No general official support assumed; use only expat/community resources unless separately verified.

labor_market.software_engineer_notes: For this selection the applicant works remotely for a foreign client. Local software market is secondary; for long-term settlement, local language, tax registration, and ability to document lawful income matter more than local hiring demand in Indonesia.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Italy does not automatically give citizenship to foreign parents’ child; the child may apply at 18 if continuously resident. A local-born child does not give father a fast passport.

passport.strength_notes: Very strong EU passport. Digital nomad visa is good for residence, but citizenship is slow unless marriage or Italian ancestry applies.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Very strong EU passport. Digital nomad visa is good for residence, but citizenship is slow unless marriage or Italian ancestry applies.

practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Naturalisation generally requires permanent residence, 5 years permanent residence, Latvian language, constitution/history/anthem knowledge, legal income and loyalty oath. Total timeline is often about 10 years.

permanent_residence_years.notes: Approximate PR/permanent-residence stage before citizenship; verify route-specific counting.

key_conditions.value: Naturalisation generally requires permanent residence, 5 years permanent residence, Latvian language, constitution/history/anthem knowledge, legal income and loyalty oath. Total timeline is often about 10 years.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.value: Long-stay visa for remote work / digital nomad visa
citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed only with certain countries/categories; verify original nationality.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The country has a useful independent route, but citizenship usually requires converting from nomad/freelance status into ordinary residence/permanent residence or proving the status counts as qualifying residence.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: No separate fast marriage citizenship track confirmed, or marriage mainly helps residence.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Latvian supports family residence but does not eliminate language and residence requirements for citizenship.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Latvia does not automatically give citizenship unless parent citizenship/statelessness rules apply.

passport.strength_notes: EU passport. Digital nomad visa is temporary; long-term plan requires converting to residence/permanent residence.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: EU passport. Digital nomad visa is temporary; long-term plan requires converting to residence/permanent residence.

visa_free_destinations.notes: Henley Passport Index 2026 visa-free / visa-on-arrival destination count.

index_notes.value: Passport rank and visa-free count filled from Henley Passport Index 2026 table.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: Local official language is important for immigration/citizenship. English may be usable in tech/tourism/expat contexts, but long-term integration normally requires the local language.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: No general official support assumed; use only expat/community resources unless separately verified.

labor_market.software_engineer_notes: For this selection the applicant works remotely for a foreign client. Local software market is secondary; for long-term settlement, local language, tax registration, and ability to document lawful income matter more than local hiring demand in Latvia.

average_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not filled with a guessed value; prompt asks for reliable source. Needs salary-source cleanup if local employment comparison becomes important.

median_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not filled with a guessed value; reliable median source not captured in this pass.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Screening summary for a foreign-contract remote worker/digital nomad. It is not tax advice. Verify official tax, social-security and immigration fee pages before relying on any amount.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Naturalisation is difficult/discretionary and typically requires long residence, Malay language and good character; DE Rantau should be treated as temporary, not a citizenship path.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Naturalisation is difficult/discretionary and typically requires long residence, Malay language and good character; DE Rantau should be treated as temporary, not a citizenship path.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is not allowed.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The route is mainly a temporary digital-nomad/workcation/visitor stay. It should not be ranked as a citizenship path unless the applicant later switches to a separate residence/PR route.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Foreign spouse routes can support long-term residence, but citizenship for spouses is discretionary and not automatic. Gender and administrative practice can matter.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Malaysia does not automatically grant citizenship to most foreign parents’ children; descent and registration rules are complex.

passport.strength_notes: Strong ASEAN passport, but citizenship is highly discretionary and dual citizenship is prohibited.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Strong ASEAN passport, but citizenship is highly discretionary and dual citizenship is prohibited.

index_notes.value: Passport rank and visa-free count filled from Henley Passport Index 2026 table.

visa_free_destinations.notes: Henley Passport Index 2026 visa-free / visa-on-arrival destination count.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: Local official language is important for immigration/citizenship. English may be usable in tech/tourism/expat contexts, but long-term integration normally requires the local language.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: No general official support assumed; use only expat/community resources unless separately verified.

labor_market.software_engineer_notes: For this selection the applicant works remotely for a foreign client. Local software market is secondary; for long-term settlement, local language, tax registration, and ability to document lawful income matter more than local hiring demand in Malaysia.

average_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not filled with a guessed value; prompt asks for reliable source. Needs salary-source cleanup if local employment comparison becomes important.

median_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not filled with a guessed value; reliable median source not captured in this pass.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Screening summary for a foreign-contract remote worker/digital nomad. It is not tax advice. Verify official tax, social-security and immigration fee pages before relying on any amount.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Ordinary naturalisation may be requested after residence periods such as 5 years, but approval is highly discretionary. Nomad residence should not be assumed to lead automatically to citizenship.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Ordinary naturalisation may be requested after residence periods such as 5 years, but approval is highly discretionary. Nomad residence should not be assumed to lead automatically to citizenship.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The stay route may be called residence, but current evidence is weak that it reliably leads to PR/citizenship. Treat as live/work option first, citizenship option only after legal confirmation.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Maltese citizen can allow citizenship after 5 years of marriage, including if separated in some cases, but checks apply.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: No major automatic benefit confirmed.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Malta does not usually give citizenship unless a parent is Maltese or qualifying former citizen/resident conditions apply.

passport.strength_notes: Very strong EU passport. Nomad permit is residence, but ordinary naturalisation is discretionary; citizenship by investment/residency is separate and expensive.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Very strong EU passport. Nomad permit is residence, but ordinary naturalisation is discretionary; citizenship by investment/residency is separate and expensive.
