total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Naturalisation generally requires 5 years residence, Spanish/culture/history exam, integration and clean record; reduced to 2 years for Mexican spouse/child, Latin American/Iberian nationals and other categories.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Naturalisation generally requires 5 years residence, Spanish/culture/history exam, integration and clean record; reduced to 2 years for Mexican spouse/child, Latin American/Iberian nationals and other categories.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.value: Temporary Resident Visa by financial solvency
citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The selected route is PR/residence-oriented or can plausibly be converted/maintained as qualifying residence for naturalisation if the applicant actually relocates and satisfies residence, language, tax and continuity rules.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Mexican can reduce citizenship timing to about 2 years residence with genuine cohabitation and documentation.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: A child born in Mexico is Mexican by jus soli; having a Mexican child can support residence and reduce naturalisation timing for the father.

passport.strength_notes: Strong regional passport. Temporary residence by solvency is one of the cleaner routes because it can lead to PR and later citizenship.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Strong regional passport. Temporary residence by solvency is one of the cleaner routes because it can lead to PR and later citizenship.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: Spanish is the practical daily/legal language; English can work in tech/expat circles, but immigration, courts and tax filings are mostly Spanish.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: No general official support assumed; use only expat/community resources unless separately verified.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is restricted except treaty/exception cases.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The stay route may be called residence, but current evidence is weak that it reliably leads to PR/citizenship. Treat as live/work option first, citizenship option only after legal confirmation.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Naturalisation generally requires 5 years residence, reduced to 3 for nationals of Spain/Latin America or those with Panamanian spouse/children, plus Spanish/civics and good conduct.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Naturalisation generally requires 5 years residence, reduced to 3 for nationals of Spain/Latin America or those with Panamanian spouse/children, plus Spanish/civics and good conduct.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.value: Short Stay Visa as Remote Worker
citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is restricted/formally complex, though practice varies.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The route is mainly a temporary digital-nomad/workcation/visitor stay. It should not be ranked as a citizenship path unless the applicant later switches to a separate residence/PR route.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Panamanian can reduce residence requirement to about 3 years, but relationship and residence must be genuine.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: A child born in Panama is Panamanian by jus soli and can reduce naturalisation timing/support family residence strategy.

passport.strength_notes: Useful passport. Remote-worker short-stay visa is not the same as permanent residence; citizenship plan needs another residence route.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Useful passport. Remote-worker short-stay visa is not the same as permanent residence; citizenship plan needs another residence route.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Updated from older 5-year rule. Current reporting indicates standard naturalisation now requires 10 years of legal residence; EU and CPLP nationals may qualify after 7 years. Clock may run from residence permit issuance, not application date, so AIMA delays can extend calendar time.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Standard naturalisation: 10 years legal residence, Portuguese language/civic knowledge requirements, clean criminal record, and other nationality-law conditions. EU/CPLP nationals may have a 7-year route. Verify official final text before filing.
key_conditions.notes: Portugal citizenship timeline corrected after 2025/2026 nationality-law reform reporting.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.value: D8 Digital Nomad / Remote Work Visa
citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed.

citizenship_processing_time.value: After eligibility, nationality processing can add additional time; administrative delays/backlogs can materially extend calendar-to-passport beyond the residence requirement.

status_transition_notes.value: D8/digital nomad residence can still be useful, but citizenship planning should assume a longer 10-year standard naturalisation timeline unless the applicant qualifies for a 7-year EU/CPLP category or another special route.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: Route remains residence-oriented, but it is no longer a fast 5-year citizenship option under current reform reporting.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage or de facto union with a Portuguese citizen for 3+ years can support nationality by declaration, but authorities can oppose if there is no effective connection or relationship is not genuine.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: A child born in Portugal can be Portuguese if a parent has legal residence or has lived there for the statutory period; a Portuguese child can strengthen family residence, but does not automatically naturalise the father.

passport.strength_notes: Very strong EU passport. D8 is one of the best nomad-to-citizenship candidates if it is converted/maintained as qualifying residence.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Very strong EU passport. D8 is one of the best nomad-to-citizenship candidates if it is converted/maintained as qualifying residence.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Ordinary naturalisation generally requires 8 years domicile/residence, reduced to 5 if married to a Romanian, plus Romanian language/culture/constitution, livelihood and good conduct.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Ordinary naturalisation generally requires 8 years domicile/residence, reduced to 5 if married to a Romanian, plus Romanian language/culture/constitution, livelihood and good conduct.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The country has a useful independent route, but citizenship usually requires converting from nomad/freelance status into ordinary residence/permanent residence or proving the status counts as qualifying residence.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Romanian reduces the required period but is not automatic; genuine relationship, residence and language/civics remain important.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Romania generally does not give citizenship unless a parent is Romanian or foundling/statelessness rules apply.

passport.strength_notes: EU passport. Digital nomad visa can start residence, but ordinary citizenship remains long and language/integration-heavy.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: EU passport. Digital nomad visa can start residence, but ordinary citizenship remains long and language/integration-heavy.

visa_free_destinations.notes: Henley Passport Index 2026 visa-free / visa-on-arrival destination count.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: Local official language is important for immigration/citizenship. English may be usable in tech/tourism/expat contexts, but long-term integration normally requires the local language.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: No general official support assumed; use only expat/community resources unless separately verified.

labor_market.software_engineer_notes: For this selection the applicant works remotely for a foreign client. Local software market is secondary; for long-term settlement, local language, tax registration, and ability to document lawful income matter more than local hiring demand in Romania.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Screening summary for a foreign-contract remote worker/digital nomad. It is not tax advice. Verify official tax, social-security and immigration fee pages before relying on any amount.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Citizenship/naturalisation is long-residence and discretionary; workcation/visitor status should not be assumed to count fully.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Citizenship/naturalisation is long-residence and discretionary; workcation/visitor status should not be assumed to count fully.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed in many cases; verify category.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The route is mainly a temporary digital-nomad/workcation/visitor stay. It should not be ranked as a citizenship path unless the applicant later switches to a separate residence/PR route.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Seychellois may support residence/naturalisation but usually after a long qualifying period and checks. No automatic citizenship by marriage.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Seychelles generally does not give citizenship unless a parent is Seychellois or special/statelessness rules apply.

passport.strength_notes: Strong African passport. Workcation is temporary and weak for citizenship unless converted to ordinary residence.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Strong African passport. Workcation is temporary and weak for citizenship unless converted to ordinary residence.

index_notes.value: Passport rank and visa-free count filled from Henley Passport Index 2026 table.

visa_free_destinations.notes: Henley Passport Index 2026 visa-free / visa-on-arrival destination count.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: English is an official or widely usable business language; remote work logistics are comparatively easy.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: No general official support assumed; use only expat/community resources unless separately verified.

labor_market.software_engineer_notes: For this selection the applicant works remotely for a foreign client. Local software market is secondary; for long-term settlement, local language, tax registration, and ability to document lawful income matter more than local hiring demand in Seychelles.

average_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not filled with a guessed value; prompt asks for reliable source. Needs salary-source cleanup if local employment comparison becomes important.

data.selection_summary: Singapore is not eligible for the stated profile: regular foreign-contract remote work. It does not offer a normal digital-nomad/foreign-client remote-work route; available routes are elite/founder/startup/high-skill or prospective Singapore employment routes such as EntrePass/ONE Pass/Tech.Pass.

rejected_routes.route_name: EntrePass / ONE Pass / Tech.Pass high-skilled founder routes

minimum_monthly_income_usd.notes: Use income_requirement_display/local formula; fixed USD monthly threshold not reliably captured unless stated there.

minimum_income_local_currency.value: ONE Pass salary route: S$30,000 fixed monthly salary for 12 consecutive months before application, or prospective Singapore employment at S$30,000/month. EntrePass: no fixed salary threshold; requires qualifying venture-backed/innovative company and criteria such as S$100,000 funding, incubator/accelerator support, IP, tech exit, or research collaboration.
minimum_income_local_currency.notes: Singapore remains valid only for elite/high-skill/founder routes, not pure foreign-contract remote work.

income_requirement_display.value: ONE Pass: S$30,000/mo for 12 consecutive months, or qualifying prospective Singapore job; EntrePass: no fixed income threshold, startup/funding/IP/incubator/research evidence instead.
income_requirement_display.local_or_formula_value: ONE Pass S$30,000/month for 12 months; EntrePass route-specific startup/innovation evidence.
income_requirement_display.notes: No normal digital nomad income threshold. Use official MOM route checklist for the chosen route.

income_proof_months.display_value: Route-specific: ONE Pass salary route requires 12 consecutive months of S$30,000 fixed monthly salary proof; EntrePass has no simple income-proof-months rule and instead requires startup/funding/IP/incubator/research evidence.
income_proof_months.notes: Do not treat Singapore as a normal digital-nomad income-proof route. Proof depends on whether applicant uses ONE Pass salary, EntrePass startup/innovation route, or another high-skill route.

key_requirements.requirement: ONE Pass salary route: at least S$30,000 fixed monthly salary for the 12 consecutive months before application, or prospective employment in Singapore at that salary; outstanding achievements can be assessed without salary criteria.
EntrePass route: operate or intend to operate a Singapore private limited company that is venture-backed or owns innovative technologies, and meet at least one founder/investor/innovator criterion.
Not a pure digital nomad route for foreign-contract remote work; Singapore requires a qualifying high-skill, founder, employment, or innovation basis.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Screening summary for a foreign-contract remote worker/digital nomad. It is not tax advice. Verify official tax, social-security and immigration fee pages before relying on any amount.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Naturalisation generally requires permanent residence and ordinarily 5 years residence after PR/qualifying residence, good character, language knowledge and intention to reside. Remote-work visa alone is not enough.

permanent_residence_years.notes: Approximate PR/permanent-residence stage before citizenship; verify route-specific counting.

key_conditions.value: Naturalisation generally requires permanent residence and ordinarily 5 years residence after PR/qualifying residence, good character, language knowledge and intention to reside. Remote-work visa alone is not enough.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed but South Africans may need retention permission before acquiring another citizenship.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The country has a useful independent route, but citizenship usually requires converting from nomad/freelance status into ordinary residence/permanent residence or proving the status counts as qualifying residence.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a South African can support spousal residence and later PR/citizenship, but citizenship still requires status, residence and checks.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in South Africa gives citizenship if at least one parent is citizen/PR; children born to foreign temporary residents may have later rights if registered and resident until majority. It does not automatically naturalise father.

passport.strength_notes: Moderate passport. Remote-work visa may be useful, but citizenship usually requires permanent residence first.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Moderate passport. Remote-work visa may be useful, but citizenship usually requires permanent residence first.

index_notes.value: Passport rank and visa-free count filled from Henley Passport Index 2026 table.

visa_free_destinations.notes: Henley Passport Index 2026 visa-free / visa-on-arrival destination count.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: English is an official or widely usable business language; remote work logistics are comparatively easy.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: No general official support assumed; use only expat/community resources unless separately verified.

labor_market.software_engineer_notes: For this selection the applicant works remotely for a foreign client. Local software market is secondary; for long-term settlement, local language, tax registration, and ability to document lawful income matter more than local hiring demand in South Africa.

average_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not filled with a guessed value; prompt asks for reliable source. Needs salary-source cleanup if local employment comparison becomes important.

median_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not filled with a guessed value; reliable median source not captured in this pass.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: General naturalisation usually requires 5 years domicile, adult capacity, good conduct, livelihood and Korean language/civic integration. Digital nomad status should not be assumed to lead to permanent residence.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: General naturalisation usually requires 5 years domicile, adult capacity, good conduct, livelihood and Korean language/civic integration. Digital nomad status should not be assumed to lead to permanent residence.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is restricted; naturalised adults often must renounce or pledge not to exercise foreign nationality, depending category.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Naturalisation processing is discretionary and can take many months to over a year after eligibility.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The route is mainly a temporary digital-nomad/workcation/visitor stay. It should not be ranked as a citizenship path unless the applicant later switches to a separate residence/PR route.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Korean can allow simplified naturalisation after 2 years marriage/residence or 3 years marriage with 1 year residence, but relationship continuity and integration are checked.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Korea does not give citizenship unless a parent is Korean or statelessness rules apply; a Korean citizen child can support family status if one parent is Korean.

passport.strength_notes: Top-tier passport. F-1-D is current and useful for stay, but not a direct settlement route; Korean language/integration and dual-citizenship restrictions matter.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Top-tier passport. F-1-D is current and useful for stay, but not a direct settlement route; Korean language/integration and dual-citizenship restrictions matter.

Professional qualifications/experience and proof of ongoing work relationship
Income, insurance/social security, background check and ordinary consular documents

initial_validity.value: Initial visa up to 1 year; residence authorization can extend longer under Spanish rules

extension_rules.value: Residence permit route can extend beyond initial visa if conditions continue

path_to_temporary_residence.value: Lawful stay/residence for granted period.

path_to_permanent_residence.notes: May count toward ordinary long-term residence/citizenship timelines if residence remains lawful; depends on nationality and continuity. path_to_citizenship.value: Potentially yes, if this status is maintained/converted as qualifying residence and all ordinary naturalisation requirements are met.
path_to_citizenship.notes: The selected route is PR/residence-oriented or can plausibly be converted/maintained as qualifying residence for naturalisation if the applicant actually relocates and satisfies residence, language, tax and continuity rules.

income_tax_rate_percent.notes: Indicative top combined IRPF rate; exact rate varies by autonomous community. Digital nomads may sometimes qualify for inbound worker/Beckham-style regime, but eligibility is technical and not automatic for all remote contractors.

tax_residency_rule.value: Spain tax residence generally arises if the person spends more than 183 days in Spain in a calendar year, has center of economic interests there, or has spouse/minor children habitually resident there.
tax_residency_rule.notes: Tax residence is calendar-year based and treaty facts matter.

foreign_income_taxation.notes: Not deeply researched in this batch; verify tax residency and treaty effects before planning.

social_security.value: Depends on whether income is treated as employment, self-employment, local business income or foreign-source remote work. Digital nomad permits often do not equal local social-insurance coverage.
social_security.notes: Needs country-specific payroll/self-employment review before choosing structure.

special_regimes.value: Progressive IRPF can reach roughly mid/high-40% combined state/regional rates; digital nomads may sometimes use the inbound worker special regime with 24% on qualifying Spanish employment income, but eligibility is technical.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Screening summary for a foreign-contract remote worker/digital nomad. It is not tax advice. Verify official tax, social-security and immigration fee pages before relying on any amount.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Default ordinary residence period is 10 years, but 2 years for nationals of Ibero-American countries, Andorra, Philippines, Equatorial Guinea, Portugal and Sephardic-origin applicants; 1 year in some family/special cases.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Spain digital nomad residence can be a real citizenship-oriented route if the applicant maintains qualifying legal residence. Default citizenship is 10 years, but it is dramatically better for Ibero-American nationals and some special categories. Requirements usually include continuous legal residence, good civic conduct, integration, DELE/CCSE unless exempt, and clean criminal records.
key_conditions.notes: Physical absence limits and whether remote-work residence remains qualifying must be managed carefully.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is limited, but Spain has special treatment for Ibero-American/Portuguese/Philippine/Equatorial Guinean/Andorran links.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 1-3+ years after filing, depending on Ministry backlog, documents, exams, appeals and nationality category.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The selected route is PR/residence-oriented or can plausibly be converted/maintained as qualifying residence for naturalisation if the applicant actually relocates and satisfies residence, language, tax and continuity rules.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: A spouse of a Spanish citizen can apply after 1 year legal residence if still married/not separated and living together; not automatic.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Spanish citizen is a major accelerator only after 1 year of legal residence in Spain and genuine cohabitation. Existing marriage to a non-Spanish spouse does not speed citizenship. Authorities can scrutinize sham marriage and require civil registration, cohabitation and clean records.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: Existing non-Spanish spouse can usually be included/dependent under residence rules, but does not reduce citizenship time. Marriage to Spanish citizen can reduce to 1 year residence if genuine and properly registered.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Spain does not automatically give citizenship in most cases, but a child born in Spain can often apply after 1 year residence; that can strengthen family strategy but does not instantly naturalise father.

passport.strength_notes: Very strong EU passport. Spain is excellent for Ibero-American nationals but slower for others; digital nomad residence can count if maintained properly.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Very strong EU passport. Spain is excellent for Ibero-American nationals but slower for others; digital nomad residence can count if maintained properly.

rank.notes: Visa requirements for Spanish citizens / Henley 2026

visa_free_destinations.notes: Visa requirements for Spanish citizens / Henley 2026

index_notes.value: Passport mobility filled from Visa requirements for Spanish citizens / Henley 2026.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: Spanish is essential for immigration, tax, banking and citizenship. English is workable in tech/expat environments in Madrid/Barcelona/Valencia/Malaga, but not enough for DELE/CCSE unless exempt.
practical_remote_work_notes.notes: Co-official regional languages may matter in Catalonia, Valencia, Balearics, Basque Country and Galicia.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: No general official support assumed; use only expat/community resources unless separately verified.

labor_market.software_engineer_notes: For this selection the applicant works remotely for a foreign client. Local software market is secondary; for long-term settlement, local language, tax registration, and ability to document lawful income matter more than local hiring demand in Spain.

average_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not filled with a guessed value; prompt asks for reliable source. Needs salary-source cleanup if local employment comparison becomes important.

median_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not filled with a guessed value; reliable median source not captured in this pass.

birthright_citizenship.notes: Spain is not unconditional jus soli. Children born in Spain to foreign parents may be Spanish by simple presumption if parents cannot transmit nationality, or may apply after 1 year residence.

benefit_to_migrant_father.value: A Spanish child can strongly support family residence/arraigo familiar. A child born in Spain to two migrants may also become Spanish after 1 year residence in many cases, but this does not instantly naturalise the father.

if_both_parents_migrants.value: If both parents are foreign migrants, the child is not automatically Spanish in most cases. If parents’ countries do not transmit citizenship, Spanish nationality by simple presumption may apply; otherwise the child can often apply after 1 year legal residence.

if_second_parent_local_citizen.value: If the other parent is Spanish, the child is Spanish by origin. That helps the migrant father’s family residence, and may support the 1-year citizenship route if married to/living with the Spanish spouse, but father is not automatic citizen.

parent_benefit_summary.value: Birth in Spain does not automatically give citizenship in most cases, but a child born in Spain can often apply after 1 year residence; that can strengthen family strategy but does not instantly naturalise father.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Citizenship by registration/naturalisation is discretionary and tied to residence, character and government approval. Digital nomad visa should be treated as temporary unless residence status can be converted.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Citizenship by registration/naturalisation is discretionary and tied to residence, character and government approval. Digital nomad visa should be treated as temporary unless residence status can be converted.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship requires approval and is not automatic.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The route is mainly a temporary digital-nomad/workcation/visitor stay. It should not be ranked as a citizenship path unless the applicant later switches to a separate residence/PR route.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Sri Lankan may support residence/registration routes but is not an automatic or especially fast passport path; local legal advice is important.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Sri Lanka does not generally give citizenship unless a parent is Sri Lankan; descent/registration rules matter.

passport.strength_notes: Moderate passport. Digital nomad visa is a temporary stay; citizenship is discretionary and dual citizenship needs approval.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Moderate passport. Digital nomad visa is a temporary stay; citizenship is discretionary and dual citizenship needs approval.

if_both_parents_migrants.value: Child normally does not receive citizenship merely by local birth if both parents are foreign temporary migrants, except statelessness/special rules.

if_second_parent_local_citizen.value: If the other parent is a local citizen, the child usually receives citizenship by descent or birth registration. This can support the migrant father’s family residence, but does not automatically grant father citizenship.

parent_benefit_summary.value: Birth in Sri Lanka does not generally give citizenship unless a parent is Sri Lankan; descent/registration rules matter.

data.selection_summary: Thailand has the Destination Thailand Visa (DTV), a long-term multiple-entry visa used by digital nomads, freelancers, and other long-stay visitors. It is a strong option: five-year visa, 180 days per entry, with a one-time in-country extension per entry reported.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Screening summary for a foreign-contract remote worker/digital nomad. It is not tax advice. Verify official tax, social-security and immigration fee pages before relying on any amount.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Naturalisation generally requires permanent residence or long lawful residence, Thai language, income/tax record, good character and often work-permit history. DTV alone is unlikely to lead directly to citizenship.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Naturalisation generally requires permanent residence or long lawful residence, Thai language, income/tax record, good character and often work-permit history. DTV alone is unlikely to lead directly to citizenship.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is tolerated in some cases but legally nuanced; verify before naturalising.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The route is mainly a temporary digital-nomad/workcation/visitor stay. It should not be ranked as a citizenship path unless the applicant later switches to a separate residence/PR route.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Thai citizen can reduce some requirements, especially for men with Thai wives after qualifying residence/income/tax history, but citizenship is still discretionary.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Thailand does not automatically give citizenship to most foreign parents’ children unless parent status/descent/statelessness rules apply.

passport.strength_notes: Moderate passport. DTV is long-stay but not designed as a PR/citizenship bridge; work-permit/tax/PR path is separate.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Moderate passport. DTV is long-stay but not designed as a PR/citizenship bridge; work-permit/tax/PR path is separate.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: Often lower risk of residence-based taxation, but local-source income, local work, PE risk, VAT, visa conditions and treaty rules still need review.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: Assume local tax filing may be required; many countries tax residents on worldwide income unless a territorial, remittance, exemption or special nomad regime applies.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: May trigger social-security, health-insurance, minimum contribution, VAT or fixed monthly/annual payments even when PIT is low.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Screening summary for a foreign-contract remote worker/digital nomad. It is not tax advice. Verify official tax, social-security and immigration fee pages before relying on any amount.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Ordinary naturalisation generally requires 5 years uninterrupted residence, intent to settle, Turkish language, good morals, income and health/security checks.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Ordinary naturalisation generally requires 5 years uninterrupted residence, intent to settle, Turkish language, good morals, income and health/security checks.
key_conditions.notes: Deep review focused on citizenship feasibility from a remote-worker/family perspective.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is allowed, subject to notification/registration rules.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.
citizenship_processing_time.notes: Screening estimate; verify official processing statistics where available.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The country has a useful independent route, but citizenship usually requires converting from nomad/freelance status into ordinary residence/permanent residence or proving the status counts as qualifying residence.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage to a Turkish citizen for 3 years can support citizenship application if living in family unity and no national-security/public-order issue; it is not automatic.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in Turkey does not automatically give citizenship unless a parent is Turkish or child would otherwise be stateless.

passport.strength_notes: Useful passport with broad regional access. Digital nomad visa is not the same as citizenship by investment or long-term residence.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Useful passport with broad regional access. Digital nomad visa is not the same as citizenship by investment or long-term residence.

permanent_residence_years.notes: No separate PR number confirmed for the nomad route; citizenship clock depends on converting to qualifying residence.

key_conditions.value: Naturalisation is exceptional/discretionary and generally requires very long residence or nomination/investor/scientist/special-talent categories. Do not choose UAE for ordinary citizenship planning.

citizenship_via_nomad_route.notes: Nomad/remote-worker status is not automatically citizenship-qualifying unless the country treats it as legal residence and it can be renewed/converted.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship is restricted and usually exceptional/approval-based.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Ordinary processing is not a predictable application timeline; citizenship is exceptional/nomination/discretion based.

status_transition_notes.value: Main risk: a digital nomad/visitor-style permit may allow temporary stay but may not count toward permanent residence or citizenship unless law treats it as qualifying legal residence and renewals/conversions are possible.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: The route is mainly a temporary digital-nomad/workcation/visitor stay. It should not be ranked as a citizenship path unless the applicant later switches to a separate residence/PR route.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Approximate minimum statutory/typical period; actual filing often also needs residence, language, good character and genuine relationship proof.

requirements_and_risks.value: Foreign women married to Emirati men may have paths after long marriage/children; foreign men married to Emirati women generally do not receive an equivalent simple citizenship path. Rules are gendered and discretionary.
requirements_and_risks.notes: Focus: whether marriage creates citizenship shortcut or mainly a residence/family route.

residence_benefit.value: No major automatic benefit confirmed.

existing_marriage_impact.value: An already existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent/family inclusion on the visa, but does not speed citizenship. Marriage to a local citizen may help residence/citizenship only if genuine, documented and compliant with residence/language rules.
existing_marriage_impact.notes: Fraud/fake marriage can create immigration refusal, cancellation, criminal or ban risks.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Authorities can request proof of cohabitation, shared finances, communication, interviews and civil-status documents. Do not model a fictitious marriage as an immigration strategy.

child.notes: Birth in UAE does not give citizenship to foreign parents’ children. Emirati citizenship is primarily descent-based, with complex gender/status rules.

passport.strength_notes: Very strong passport, but citizenship is highly exceptional. UAE remote-work residence is not a realistic citizenship path.

citizenship_planning_notes.value: Very strong passport, but citizenship is highly exceptional. UAE remote-work residence is not a realistic citizenship path.

dual_citizenship.value: Dual citizenship/nationality is tolerated; Uruguay distinguishes nationality and legal citizenship.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 6-36+ months after eligibility, depending on documents, backlog, security checks, language/civics exams and discretion.

residence_benefit.value: Can help residence and/or reduce citizenship timing, but not automatic.

benefit_to_migrant_father.value: A child born in Uruguay is Uruguayan by jus soli and is a strong family anchor; it can support the 3-year family-established citizenship route for the father.
benefit_to_migrant_father.notes: This is about whether a locally born child helps the migrant father; it is not legal advice.

if_both_parents_migrants.value: Child normally receives citizenship by birth in the country, except narrow diplomatic/other exceptions. This can materially improve family residence strategy for parents.

if_second_parent_local_citizen.value: If the other parent is a local citizen, the child usually receives citizenship by descent or birth registration. This can support the migrant father’s family residence, but does not automatically grant father citizenship.

parent_benefit_summary.value: A child born in Uruguay is Uruguayan by jus soli and is a strong family anchor; it can support the 3-year family-established citizenship route for the father.

deep_family_citizenship_review.scope: 42 valid_for_selection countries only: citizenship timeline, marriage shortcut/risk, child citizenship impact, dual-citizenship risk.
deep_family_citizenship_review.warning: Use as screening data. Before acting, verify with official nationality law/immigration counsel because residence-counting and family rules change and can be discretionary.

settlement_track.summary: The selected route is PR/residence-oriented or can plausibly be converted/maintained as qualifying residence for naturalisation if the applicant actually relocates and satisfies residence, language, tax and continuity rules.

full_match_recheck.notes: Structural recheck only: required dashboard fields are present. Legal/tax decisions still require source-level review before filing.

data.selection_summary: Uzbekistan IT Visa remains route-specific rather than a generic digital-nomad visa: it is for IT Park resident-company employees/specialists, founders and investors. Special IT Park taxation is attractive if the structure qualifies: PIT 7.5% for residents, and 0% corporate/social/VAT/property-land taxes for IT Park residents per IT Park table.

minimum_monthly_income_usd.notes: Use income_requirement_display/local formula; fixed USD monthly threshold not reliably captured unless stated there.

minimum_income_local_currency.value: IT specialist: at least USD 30,000 income from IT activities for the last 12 months. Investor: at least USD 10,000 financing to an IT legal entity. Founder: founder of an IT Park resident legal entity. Not a generic nomad income formula.
minimum_income_local_currency.notes: Kept in local/formula field because requirement is route-specific and stated in USD by IT Park.

income_requirement_display.value: IT specialist: USD 30,000 IT income in last 12 months; investor: USD 10,000 financing; founder: IT Park resident company founder. Route is IT Park-specific, not generic digital nomad.
income_requirement_display.local_or_formula_value: USD 30,000 IT income / last 12 months for specialist route; USD 10,000 investment for investor route.
income_requirement_display.notes: A resident company is eligible to apply for IT Visas for founders, investors and employees. Regular foreign-contract remote work outside IT Park resident structure is not enough.

income_proof_months.display_value: IT specialist route: prove at least USD 30,000 income from IT activities for the last 12 months. Founder/investor routes use IT Park resident/investment criteria instead.
income_proof_months.notes: The official IT Visa page describes the IT specialist requirement as employment in an IT Park resident legal entity plus USD 30,000 IT income for the last 12 months.

key_requirements.requirement: IT specialist: employed in an IT specialty by a legal entity resident of Uzbekistan/IT Park and confirms at least USD 30,000 income from IT activities for the last 12 months.
Investor: financing to a legal entity operating in IT under an agreement of at least USD 10,000.
Founder: founder/participant of a legal entity registered as an IT Park resident.
IT Park resident tax incentives can reduce personal income tax to 7.5% and set several resident-company taxes/social tax to 0%, but this depends on IT Park resident status and structure.

path_to_temporary_residence.value: Potential lawful temporary residence/stay if route criteria are met.

path_to_permanent_residence.notes: Depends on conversion to ordinary residence/PR route; not automatic.

path_to_citizenship.value: Possible only if qualifying residence/PR path is maintained or converted.
path_to_citizenship.notes: Detailed re-check: Uzbekistan is upgraded to valid for an IT specialist/founder profile, but not as a general digital nomad visa for any foreign employee.

best_routes.notes: Detailed re-check: Uzbekistan is upgraded to valid for an IT specialist/founder profile, but not as a general digital nomad visa for any foreign employee.

minimum_monthly_income_usd.notes: No simple remote-worker salary threshold; eligibility depends on IT Park/specialist/founder/investor criteria.

minimum_income_local_currency.notes: No simple remote-worker salary threshold; eligibility depends on IT Park/specialist/founder/investor criteria.

key_requirements.requirement: IT specialist/founder/investor/professor or qualifying IT Park/startup role
Meet IT Park/Ministry eligibility documents
Family members may be covered under source summary

initial_validity.value: Multiple-entry visa up to 3 years, extendable in source summary

path_to_temporary_residence.value: Lawful stay/residence for granted period.

path_to_permanent_residence.notes: Not confirmed from this route in this pass.

path_to_citizenship.notes: Not confirmed from this route in this pass.

best_routes.notes: Promising for tech profile but needs official IT Park confirmation; not a pure nomad visa.

income_tax_rate_percent.notes: Special IT Park resident personal income tax rate. Standard non-resident/ordinary PIT screening rate remains 12%; 7.5% should only be used if the applicant fits IT Park resident/IT Visa structure.

tax_residency_rule.value: Usually day-count/center-of-interests/residence-permit based; confirm before exceeding 183 days or registering a business locally.

foreign_income_taxation.notes: Not deeply researched in this batch; verify tax residency and treaty effects before planning.

social_security.value: IT Park resident comparison table shows social tax 0% for residents versus 12% for non-residents. Individual pension/insurance/payroll treatment still depends on employment and resident-company payroll setup.
social_security.notes: Do not assume all social/insurance obligations are zero outside IT Park resident payroll/company structure.

taxation_system.value: Special IT Park regime: PIT 7.5% for residents under the IT Park table, compared with ordinary 12%; resident-company taxes/social tax/VAT/property-land tax are shown as 0%. This is route- and status-specific, not a generic digital nomad regime.
taxation_system.tax_residency_rule: Usually day-count/center-of-interests/residence-permit based; confirm before exceeding 183 days or registering a business locally.
taxation_system.income_scope: Special IT Park tax treatment depends on IT Park resident structure. Foreign-contract remote income outside an IT Park resident company may be ordinary taxable income and needs separate review.
taxation_system.special_regimes: IT Park resident incentives: personal income tax 7.5%; corporate taxes 0%; social tax 0%; property and land tax 0%; VAT 0%; VAT on import of IT services 0%; no customs payments for own-needs imports per IT Park comparison table. Applies only if the structure qualifies as IT Park resident/IT Visa route.
taxation_system.social_security: IT Park resident comparison table shows social tax 0% for residents versus 12% for non-residents. Individual pension/insurance/payroll treatment still depends on employment and resident-company payroll setup.
taxation_system.notes: Use 7.5% only for the IT Park/IT Visa structure. For ordinary remote work outside the regime, use ordinary Uzbekistan tax review.

digital_nomad_taxation.value: Uzbekistan IT Visa special taxation: IT Park resident table shows PIT 7.5% instead of ordinary 12%, plus 0% corporate taxes, social tax, property/land tax, VAT and VAT on imported IT services for IT Park residents. This is not a generic digital-nomad tax break; it depends on IT Park resident company / employee / founder / investor status.
digital_nomad_taxation.income_tax_display: IT Park resident PIT 7.5%; ordinary/non-resident comparison shown as 12% on IT Park page. Resident-company incentives include 0% corporate tax, social tax, property/land tax, VAT and VAT on imported IT services according to IT Park table.
digital_nomad_taxation.tax_residency_rule: Usually day-count/center-of-interests/residence-permit based; confirm before exceeding 183 days or registering a business locally.
digital_nomad_taxation.foreign_income_treatment: IT Park regime is structure-specific. Ordinary foreign-contract income outside IT Park resident structure may not qualify for 7.5%; verify tax residence and source rules.
digital_nomad_taxation.special_digital_nomad_or_inbound_regime: IT Park resident incentives: personal income tax 7.5%; corporate taxes 0%; social tax 0%; property and land tax 0%; VAT 0%; VAT on import of IT services 0%; no customs payments for own-needs imports per IT Park comparison table. Applies only if the structure qualifies as IT Park resident/IT Visa route.
digital_nomad_taxation.social_security_and_payroll: IT Park resident comparison table shows social tax 0% for residents versus 12% for non-residents. Individual pension/insurance/payroll treatment still depends on employment and resident-company payroll setup.

fixed_payments.social_security_or_payroll: IT Park page shows social tax 0% for residents vs 12% non-residents; payroll/individual obligations require structure-specific verification.
fixed_payments.visa_or_state_fees: Not captured on the tax page; check IT Visa application portal for visa/state fees.
fixed_payments.other_fixed_or_transaction_taxes: IT Park resident table shows corporate taxes, property/land tax, VAT and VAT on imported IT services at 0%; customs payments for own-needs imports marked as NO for residents.
fixed_payments.notes: The attractive tax treatment is tied to IT Park resident status, not merely being a remote IT contractor physically in Uzbekistan.

practical_scenarios.under_tax_residency_threshold: May avoid ordinary tax residence depending on facts, but IT Visa/IT Park payroll status can still create local reporting/payroll issues.
practical_scenarios.tax_resident_remote_worker: If outside IT Park resident structure, do not assume 7.5%; ordinary tax rules may apply. If inside IT Park structure, check eligibility for the 7.5% PIT regime.
practical_scenarios.self_employed_or_local_registration: IT Park resident company/founder route can access special resident-company tax incentives, but requires qualifying IT Park resident status.

digital_nomad_taxation.notes: Special taxation added after official IT Park source check.

income_tax_rate_display.value: IT Park resident PIT 7.5%; ordinary/non-resident comparison shown as 12% on IT Park page. Resident-company incentives include 0% corporate tax, social tax, property/land tax, VAT and VAT on imported IT services according to IT Park table.
income_tax_rate_display.notes: This is a special IT Park regime, not a generic digital nomad tax holiday.

special_regimes.value: IT Park resident incentives: personal income tax 7.5%; corporate taxes 0%; social tax 0%; property and land tax 0%; VAT 0%; VAT on import of IT services 0%; no customs payments for own-needs imports per IT Park comparison table. Applies only if the structure qualifies as IT Park resident/IT Visa route.
special_regimes.notes: Verify exact resident-company status, payroll setup and whether the individual income qualifies before relying on the reduced PIT rate.

total_years_to_citizenship.notes: Citizenship/naturalisation is separate and dual citizenship is restrictive; IT Visa should be treated as residence/work facilitation first.

key_conditions.value: Citizenship/naturalisation is separate and dual citizenship is restrictive; IT Visa should be treated as residence/work facilitation first.
key_conditions.notes: Updated during deep review of previously uncertain countries.

citizenship_route_realism.notes: Detailed re-check: Uzbekistan is upgraded to valid for an IT specialist/founder profile, but not as a general digital nomad visa for any foreign employee.

citizenship_processing_time.value: Often 1-3+ years after eligibility where citizenship is available; discretionary or pending routes may have no predictable citizenship processing timeline.

years_to_citizenship_via_marriage.notes: Country-specific reduced periods may exist; verify before relying.

requirements_and_risks.value: Marriage can support residence/family ties but no automatic citizenship.

existing_marriage_impact.value: Existing marriage to a non-citizen spouse usually helps dependent inclusion only; marriage to a local citizen matters only if genuine and documented.

genuine_marriage_warning.value: Fake marriage can cause refusal, cancellation, bans or criminal consequences.

child.notes: Mainly descent-based; birth alone to foreign temporary residents is not a shortcut.

index_notes.value: Passport rank and visa-free count filled from Henley Passport Index 2026 table.

visa_free_destinations.notes: Henley Passport Index 2026 visa-free / visa-on-arrival destination count.

practical_remote_work_notes.value: For immigration, tax and citizenship, local official language matters. English may work in tech/startup contexts but is not enough for long-term integration in most cases.

russian_ukrainian_notes.value: Russian/Ukrainian usefulness varies by country/community; not assumed for official processes.

labor_market.software_engineer_notes: Route reviewed for an independent software engineer. In Uzbekistan, validity depends on whether the applicant fits the route as remote worker, founder, IT specialist or self-employed person without local employer sponsorship.

average_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not researched in this pass.

median_salary_usd_monthly.notes: Not researched in this pass.
